reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. `Finset.range (n - 1)` with the function `1/(i + 2)` produces exactly the terms 1/2, 1/3, ..., 1/n (when i ranges from 0 to n-2). The hypothesis `2 ≤ n` makes explicit the natural implicit premise that the sum notation "1/2 + 1/3 + ... + 1/n" requires to be well-defined (when n=2 the sum is the single term 1/2). The conclusion `¬ ∃ a : ℤ, sum = (a : ℚ)` is the standard encoding of "the rational sum is not an integer". The logical content, types (ℚ for the sum, ℤ for the candidate integer via coercion), and quantifier structure all match the informal statement.
